trend_name: Get the Daily Beauty Blast Newsletter
description:
That's right: Instead of blown-out, flawlessly heat-styled feet of hair, Robbie is keeping the bob trend going strong with slightly textured, style that lands between her chin and shoulders. (You can call it a lob, if you insist.) HairstylistBryce Scarlettgave her a side part that gives way to imperfectly piece-y bangs that grow a bit longer at the temples, and she kept one side of her hair tucked behind an ear.
As if her loose jeans, sheer, oversized tank, and lacy bra didn't instantly guarantee cool-girl vibes, this new, significantly shorter hairstyle took it over the top. (And it seems literally cool, too, considering how lightweight that blouse looks, not to mention how much of a breeze she can now feel at the back of her neck.)
One of the most fun things about being a celebrity—I assume—is access to the ability to quickly change up one's hair length, so it wouldn't surprise us if she happened to show up at Sunday's Oscars with Rapunzel tresses again. But the fact that she's got this badass bob underneath it all makes it that much more fun.
More celebrity hair news:

description:
Styles may not have elaborated on his hair situation for theRoyal Courtappearance, but hehastalked about it in the past. In 2022,he toldRolling Stonethat he was aware of the discourse surrounding his luscious brunette hair, noting that a friend brought the DeuxMoi rumors to his attention at the time. “He won’t stop sending me messages about [people] trying to work out if I’m bald.”
He then told writer Brittany Spanos that he wasnotbald. “What is it with baldness? … It skips a generation or something, right? If your granddad’s bald then you’ll be bald? Well, my granddad wasn’t bald, so fingers crossed.”

trend_name: Chappell Roan's Sculpted Updo Transcends Time
description:
Chappell Roanmay have a signature look—those long, flowing red curls, obviously—but that doesn't mean she's sticking to that hairstyle forever and ever. Quite the opposite, actually, especially during her visit to Paris Fashion Week! The Grammy-winning musician has only been in Paris for a few days and she's changed up her hairstyle in the most incredible ways, aided by hairstylistLacy Redway. At Acne, she channeled the internet-driven “weird art school girl” vibe witha faux bob. At Rick Owens,an architectural ponytail. And at Mugler, Roan took us back in time to a few distinct eras, all beautifully remixed intoone sculptural updo.
For the show, Redway created what she called a “diva roll” for the pop star, pulling her red hair up into a series of tight rolls reminiscent of 1940s Victory rolls. The front portions were separated in hair and rolled in a horizontal direction toward the center parting. The back portion was also rolled, but vertically, and pinned to the back of the star's head. It's giving 1940s, but it's also got a bit of Regency romanticism to it, especially those wavy bangs brushing Roan's forehead. Roan's high-necked dress was also equal parts retro and timeless, with bold '40s-style shoulders and a fishtail midi skirt.
Roan's makeup was decidedly more modern, however, with makeup artist Andrew Dahling crafting a classic Chappell Roan glam for the show: sculpted cheeks with lots of blush; thick strokes of black liner on her top and bottom lashes; and slim, arched brows.
Trending Now
A Full Day On Set of 'The Buccaneers' Season 2, From Makeup to Filming
Paris Fashion Week has been a buffet of beauty inspiration, with Roan and her fellow stars giving us tons of ideas for future looks. Willow Smith'sgold metallic lipwas a standout, as was Anya Taylor-Joy'sswingy, princessy ponytail, not to mentionOprah's bubble ponyfrom the Chloé show. Can it be Paris Fashoin Week forever?
More great reads:
Now, watchBridgertonstar Yerin Ha discuss her beauty looks in the show:
FollowAllureonInstagramandTikTok, orsubscribe to our newsletterto stay up to date on all things beauty.

trend_name: Get the Daily Beauty Blast Newsletter
description:
Giannetos styled the bob to look as though Moore just stepped out of the shower or had a dip in the pool, with lots of wet, piece-y texture. When paired with her black leather jacket, pants, and face-shielding sunglasses, the look was futuristic but also a little Y2K, like Moore was hanging out on the set ofThe Matrix.In short, it fit right in with Demna's design aesthetic.
Trending Now
Brittany Broski's 10-Minute Routine for a 1930s Look
“I wanted to give Demi a very bold and fashion forward look for Demna’s show. It is inspired by the silhouettes of the new collection," Giannetos shared. “I felt an amazing, short, above-the-shoulder bob with this very sleek wet texture would be perfect to complement her look. It’s a big change for Demi—we’ve never seen her like this before! She looks very cool, effortless and modern. And it highlights her personality.”
To replicate the look, you'll needa good hair gelfor that defined, damp effect—but you'll have to find your own version of Pilaf the dog.
Moore is a Gucci gal, so we weren't surprised to see her sitting front row at the show. Last year, she even starred in a short film for the brand, playing a character named Barbara Gucci; Barbara Gucci didn't have a bob, butshedidhave curtain bangs!While we highly doubt that Moore has permanently ditched her long hair, it's so fun to see her change things up in such a major way. Wait… DoIwant a bob now?

trend_name: Get the Daily Beauty Blast Newsletter
description:
But it was her sharp bob, styled byJamika Wilson, that literally and figuratively topped off the look. Styled perfectly straight with blunt bangs, the subtly highlighted style couldn't have looked more glamorous. And it's not as intimidating as it may look, according to hairstylistOlya Iudina, whopreviously toldAllure, “If I have a client who wants to cut a bob the first time, I would recommend this one, since it’s simple and very easy to style.”
No matter how amazing she looked, Davis knows it's downright gauche for an A-lister to wear exactly the same hairstyle to another award show just 24 hours later. But even though just a little adjustment would have been fine, she went in a completely different direction for the Actor Awards while still staying decidedly in bob territory.
Wearing a gorgeous, off-the-shoulder, teal gown, Davis switched to a wavy A-line bob, meaning it's a little shorter in the back than in the front. Wilson gave it soft yet sculptural waves that conveyed the retro 1920s and 1930s theme of the night, deeply parting it to the side to give it some extra drama.
“This style adds structure and drama without sacrificing elegance, making it a favorite for those who want a modern yet timeless look,” stylistRogerio Cavalcantepreviously toldAllure. “Ask your stylist for a precise, graduated cut with a clean angle from back to front. Whether worn straight or with a slight wave, the A-line bob always delivers a bold, sophisticated edge.”
And there's no clearer proof of that than Viola Davis.

trend_name: Get the Daily Beauty Blast Newsletter
description:
These days, Palmer, 32, is embracing a new chapter—one that centers authenticity and flexibility in every area of her life. Fittingly, it comes with a new-ish hair moment: a spunkypixie cutthat the actor debuted in August 2025. Ahead, she opens up about the big chop, her decision to go back to a relaxer, and the beauty rituals she’s now passing down to her toddler.
Trending Now
A Full Day On Set of 'The Buccaneers' Season 2, From Makeup to Filming
Allure:You’ve had your pixie for about seven months now. What inspired the bold chop?
Keke Palmer:For the past year or so, I’ve just been doing everything to my hair—especially with having the Creme of Nature products at my will—while also trying to keep it healthy with the color, and just keep a consistent hair care routine. And then I hit a moment whereI became tired of maintaining my hair. I wanted something that matches the pace of my life.
I don't want to be bald yet. So the pixie lets me keep the flavor, style, funk, but at the same time, I can keep up with it day to day. Right now, I have this kind of boy band type of thing going on that I'm living for, where the ends are just spiked. I'm just having a ball with that.
Allure:How do you maintain the look?
Palmer:I get the sides and back cut every two weeks. I let the top grow out and get a refresher on my relaxer every four weeks.
I know a lot of people feel like the pixie is difficult, but if you have a good foam, some good edge control to add up in there and give it texture, and you're not afraid of letting some of your texture shine through, then you can enjoy your pixie cut and not be afraid to touch it.
Allure:Why did you decide to get a relaxer again, and what was that experience like for you?
Palmer:I've gone through the whole natural hair thing. I was doing that all last year, where I was really wanting to wear my ‘fro, especially after I didNope.I was just flexing the natural look and playing with my hair in its most natural state. And I really did enjoy that. And then I decided I want to be flexible with something else, and I don't want to be chained to anything.
I want to be able to just be me. Right now, I'm India Arie. Tomorrow, I'm this. The next day, I'm that. It’s about freedom and flexibility. I'm here for women being able to do what they want. I think a lot of times, as a Black woman, the world is constantly telling you what you need to do and how you need to look to be beautiful, and I felt the weight of that earlier this year.
I was constantly wearing wigs, always having myedges glued down, and I was getting tired of that. Now, I’m leaning towards effortlessness, and that came in the form of a pixie cut and arelaxer. I don't want to flex long hair right now. I want to flex my face and my energy. I want people to see my whole vibe when I walk into the room.
Allure:How do you decide exactly what version of Keke you want to be on any given day?
Palmer:I base it on what I'm trying to say or evoke. I also let what I’m working on guide me. Whether I'm doing something that involves music, my personality, a photoshoot where I'm being more sexy, or whatever the case may be. I let that guide me. I also like to collaborate with the stylist I'm working with and get playful with it.
I think right now, my overall vibe is very focused on being free and being precise and authentic. And I think that's why I've been leaning so much into the pixie cut, because I think there's nothing that's more precise or has to be as accurate as a cut. It also feels freeing, because I'm not bound by the idea of what it means to havelong hair.
Allure:What beauty and grooming traditions are you excited to pass down to your son?
Palmer:I love when we get our hair time, whether it's him getting it done by his hairstylist or me doing it. I just love it because it also makes him feel good to know that he's being taken care of. His routine is everything. And I think that's important, not just for babies, but for moms too, to have that moment of self-care with our hair. So I’ve definitely passed down that ritual of washing, conditioning, andprotective styles.
He goes in and out ofbraidsand then his ‘fro. I just use the same products I’m using myself, on him. But teaching him how to keep that hair scarf on is a freaking whole journey. He doesn't want to keep that on, girl. I need a baby durag line fast.
Read more celebrity interviews:
Now, watch Dove Cameron's beauty routine:

trend_name: Kerry Washington's Blonde Braids Have Me in a Spring State of Mind
description:
Kerry Washingtonmust have known that I desperately needed a mood boost after a March snowstorm, because she brought the springtime sunshine to the red carpet withthe prettiest dimensional blonde braids.
Washington was in New York City for a SAG-AFTRA Foundation Conversations event for her new Apple TV seriesImperfect Women,speaking alongside her costars Kate Mara and Elisabeth Moss. For the event, the actor wore her hair in long, textured braids that incorporated multiple shades of blonde and brunette hues, including some very bright, chunky highlights woven throughout. Washington's hair was parted in the center and loosely braided into two portions, one of which she wore over her shoulder, brushing the strap of her black tank top with a cool belted detail. And these weren't just blonde-streaked braids—they were also very long braids, flowing all the way down to her hips.
This is definitely one of Washington'sblondestlooks in awhile, with shades of gold, wheat, strawberry, and honey blonde in the mix. The warm tones in the braids absolutely glowed, especially when paired with a striking pop of more dramatic color from the super-blonde, nearly platinum color in the mid-lengths, which blended with chocolate and espresso brunette tones for a cool color contrast. Soft, airy curls escaped the braids on either side of her face and in the mid-lengths, adding a romantic, springy texture.
Trending Now
Dove Cameron's 10-Minute 'Less Is More' Beauty Routine
If you're in the mood for a spring refresh of your own, we feel you—and we've been busy trend hunting forthe best spring hair color and cutsto get you started. Maybe you fancy a “modern shag” or some long waterfall waves to ring in spring? For those vibing with Washington's warm blonde color blend, may we suggest Hamptons blonde, a buttery, golden-honey color that feels like soft sunshine, or the rooty, lived-in-cool of caramel beige? 'Tis the season to brighten up your color and have a little fun with your look!
Some of our fave Oscar night looks:
Now, watch Alysa Liu get ready:

trend_name: Get the Daily Beauty Blast Newsletter
description: Comb through the list ofBest of Beauty Hair winnersand you’re certain to find something new to add to your routine. And when you do, regardless of how you like to wear your hair, every day going forward will be a good one.

trend_name: Best of Beauty Awards 2025: Hair Care
description: Shop your favorite category or keep scrolling for the year's very best shampoos, curl creams, and dry shampoos.

trend_name: Best Shampoo & Conditioners
description: Best Fine Hair Shampoo & Conditoner

trend_name: John Frieda UltraFiller+ Shampoo and Conditioner
description:
Amazon (Shampoo)
Amazon (Conditioner)
Why it won:John Frieda UltraFiller+ Shampoo and Conditionergently cleanse and soften limp strands while staying weightless thanks to the featherlight hydrator panthenol that’s featured in both formulas. But it’s biotin and hyaluronic acid that get to the root of the situation, strengthening and hydrating hair so it’s healthier, more resilient—and fuller—overall.
Tester feedback from deputy beauty directorElizabeth Siegel
“This shampoo and conditioner do everything I want—making my fine, limp hair look deceptively fuller, shinier, and softer—without anything I don't want, like a strong perfume-y scent.”—Elizabeth Siegel, deputy beauty director
Best Dry Hair Shampoo & Conditioner

trend_name: Herbal Essences Honey Daily Moisture Shampoo & Conditioner
description:
Amazon (Shampoo)
Amazon (Conditioner)
Why It Won:You can make excuses for your parched hair—your age, your parents, your proclivity for your flatiron—or you can take action by addingHerbal Essences Honey Daily Moisture Shampoo & Conditionerto your shower. The camellia oil in the nourishing duo closely mimics the natural oils found on healthy hair and helps replenish it every time you decide to take your “everything shower.”
Tester feedback from senior beauty editorJesa Marie Calaor
“This shampoo and conditioner smell so fresh and sweet—but not cloying—and leave my hair less frizzy and so much softer.”—Jesa Marie Calaor, senior beauty editor
Best Damaged Hair Shampoo & Conditioner

trend_name: Kérastase Première Bain Décalcifiant Réparateur and Première Fondant Fluidité Réparateur
description:
Amazon (Shampoo)
Amazon (Conditioner)
Why It Won:Calcium might be good for your bones, but not for your hair when it’s coming out of your showerhead. Too much can form a film around the cuticle, which can actually prevent reparative ingredients from doing their job.Kérastase Première Bain Décalcifiant Réparateur and Première Fondant Fluidité Réparateurtackle the issue with citric acid to remove the calcium buildup from hard water, and then hand it off to glycine, a humectant that works overtime to soften the hair and make it stronger and more elastic.
Tester feedback from contributing writerSophia Panych
“I know this sounds crazy—and maybe I imagined it—but you can feel this shampoo and conditioner working as you’re in the shower. Your hair starts to feel silkier and less flimsy as you slide your comb through it, which I do in the shower as I rinse out conditioner. But it’s really how your hair looks after a couple of weeks of using this duo where you notice a visible change. My frayed ends started to look polished, and the broken bits along my hairline stopped being so fuzzy after blow-drying them.”— Sophia Panych, contributing writer
Best Color-Treated Hair Shampoo & Conditioner

trend_name: Biolage Professional Hair Spa Color Last Shampoo & Conditioner
description:
Amazon (Shampoo)
Amazon (Conditioner)
Why It Won:TheBiolage Professional Hair Spa Color Last Shampoo & Conditionerrely on soybean oil and stearic acid to keep highlights sparkly, brunettes glossy, and reds fiery for weeks, making it a bit more feasible to stretch out the time between your salon appointments. And with the brand’s signature clean-floral scent, every shower will transport you back to being in your colorist’s chair.
Tester feedback from senior director of audience developmentLexi Herrick
“This Biolage shampoo-and-conditioner combo has become one of my absolute staples. Its quality is unmatched, and every moment of applying this product screams salon luxury. It provides a thorough clean and deep-conditioning, smells amazing, and brightens my blonde almost instantly. As someone who has been actively trying to delay my highlights appointments, I can visibly see this product extending the life of my color over time.”—Lexi Herrick, senior director of audience development.
Best Curly Hair Shampoo

trend_name: Pattern Hydration Shampoo
description:
Creative producer Sydney Malone applies thePattern Hydration Shampoo.
Why It Won: Pattern Hydration Shampoowraps curls in a thirst-quenching cocoon of aloe vera leaf juice, honey, shea butter, and coconut oil. And while it offers serious hydration (and a heavenly woody-floral scent that we can’t get enough of), it’s never at the expense of shape or springiness.
Tester feedback from creative producer Sydney Malone
“This shampoo lathers well and has the perfect mild scent. I also love that it doesn't dry out and tangle my curls while I'm washing my hair. Once I'm done washing my hair, it creates the perfect base for a well-moisturized style.”—Sydney Malone, creative producer
Best Dandruff Shampoo

trend_name: Head & Shoulders Bare Soothing Hydration Shampoo
description:
Amazon (2-Pack)
Why It Won: Head & Shoulders Bare Soothing Hydration Shampoostill tackles dandruff with fungal-fighting zinc pyrithione, but in a stripped-back formula that’s free of sulfates, silicones, and dyes. What does it contain? Plant-derived cleaners and moisturizers, and a citric acid pH balancer for a gentle cleanse and a happy scalp.
Tester feedback from social directorKassidy Silva
“When my scalp is flaring up, I use this shampoo two to three times a week in my shower routine, and the relief is instant.”—Kassidy Silva, social director
Best Purple Shampoo & Conditioner

trend_name: milk_shake Icy Blond Shampoo and Conditioner
description:
Why It Won:If your blonde ambitions areHouse of the Dragon–levels of platinum, themilk_shake Icy Blond Shampoo and Conditionerwill be your most-trusted ally. The pair’s purple pigments are so dark, they’re basically black, completely banishing yellow and brassy tones, as milk proteins deeply condition. Even our bronde and highlighted testers found it gave their golden pieces a brightening boost.
Tester feedback from content marketing manager Hannah Brower
“I use this set every three weeks when my hair is looking a little bleh. It instantly brings my blonde back to life! Most purple products can be drying, but this duo nourishes while it brightens.”—Hannah Brower, content marketing manager
Best Kinky Hair Shampoo

trend_name: Creme of Nature Argan Oil from Morocco Sulfate-Free Moisture & Shine Shampoo
description:
Why It Won:If your hair texture falls within the Type 3 and 4 category, you know that your coils need all the moisture and none of the sulfates.Creme of Nature Argan Oil from Morocco Sulfate-Free Moisture & Shine Shampoo—which also skips alcohol, mineral oil, and petrolatum, by the way—relies on one of the richest hydrators out there to keep strands ridiculously soft and pliable: argan oil.
Tester feedback from creative director Amber Venerable
“This shampoo lathers so well with the tiniest drop and cleanses gently without stripping my hair of necessary oils."—Amber Venerable, creative director
Best Clarifying Shampoo

trend_name: Dae Desert Detox Clarifying Shampoo
description:
Why It Won:We appreciate having a shampoo that does some seriously deep cleaning in our routine, but only if it doesn’t leave our scalp feeling as dry as the Sahara. TheDae Desert Detox Clarifying Shampoomay include plant extracts that thrive in dry environments—like cactus flower, devil’s claw, and prickly pear seed—but that’s what makes them so good at taking care of your hair and scalp as the luscious lather gets to work breaking up buildup.
Tester feedback from shopping market editorAngela Trakoshis
“If you want squeaky clean hair with shine, this is for you.”—Angela Trakoshis, shopping market editor

trend_name: Best Co-Wash
description: Best Co-Wash

trend_name: Aveda Be Curly Advanced Co-Wash
description:
Associate visuals editor Giancarlos Kunhardt applies theAveda Be Curly Advanced Co-Wash.
Why It Won:A cleansing conditioner is critical for keeping curls, kinks, and coils happy and healthy, andAveda Be Curly Advanced Co-Washis just what thedoctorhairstylist ordered. The creamy lotion washes away everything it needs to (dirt and impurities) and nothing that your hair wants (lipid-rich natural oils), leaving behind plant-derived peptides to keep curls strong and enough nourishing kokum seed butter for no-stress detangling.
Tester feedback from associate visuals editor Giancarlos Kunhardt
“I’ve been using this co-wash for about a year now, and it’s become a tried-and-true staple in my routine. It cleans just enough between my biweekly clarifying shampoos without shocking or drying my hair. It feels like a hydrating conditioner but actually cleans my scalp and strands—key for my curly hair, which can get unruly. This formula keeps it calm and peachy, and it also works great as a topper after a harsh clarifying shampoo session. Oh, and it smells lovely!”—Giancarlos Kunhardt, associate visuals editor

trend_name: Best Treatment for Thinning Hair
description: Best Treatment for Thinning Hair

trend_name: Women's Rogaine 5% Minoxidil Unscented Foam
description:
Why It Won:We don’t mess around when it comes to thinning hair, and neither doesWomen's Rogaine 5% Minoxidil Unscented Foam,which contains the only FDA-approved, over-the-counter topical ingredient proven to regrow hair for those experiencing hereditary hair loss. And because the unscented foam has a thin consistency that all but vanishes from strands, you also get mess-free styling with whatever gel, cream, or spray you follow up with.
Tester feedback from senior news editorNicola Dall'Asen
"After losing a ton of my hair to a sudden hormonal imbalance (thanks, birth control), I've tried just about every hair growth remedy—both homeopathic and prescription—out there. Women's Rogaine 5% Minoxidil Unscented Foam is just about the only thing that I can guarantee will help you grow new hair. But you have to be consistent; this formula requires daily application to do its thing."—Nicola Dall'Asen, senior news editor

trend_name: Best Scalp Treatment
description: Best Scalp Scrub

trend_name: AAVRANI Scalp Detox Jelly Cleanser
description:
Why It Won:To loosen flakes and break up product buildup,AAVRANI Scalp Detox Jelly Cleanserskips the gritty bits in favor of skin-sloughing glycolic acid and a built-in scrubber that gently exfoliates your scalp with its nubby silicone bristles. You still get that squeaky-clean feeling, just without the hassle of rinsing out sugar crystals or cleaning out your drain catcher.
Tester feedback from features directorDianna Singh
“When a high-performing formula meets a high-performing applicator, beauty magic happens—and AAVRANI Scalp Detox Jelly Cleanser is case in point. Its silicone applicator has soft teeth that make it easy to distribute the clarifying shampoo into every nook and cranny of your scalp.”—Dianna Singh, features director
Best Scalp Tonic

trend_name: Act+Acre 3% Stem Cell Peptide Treatment
description:
Why It Won:We wouldn't fault you for thinking theAct+Acre 3% Stem Cell Peptide Treatmentis a fancy-pants serum for your face. The formula uses tried-and-true skin-care ingredients to boost collagen—but to improve hair quality, not for a plumper complexion. In addition to grape stem cells, the fast-absorbing spray includes caffeine and antioxidant-rich arugula leaf extract to help keep fallout at bay.
Tester feedback from associate visuals editor Giancarlos Kunhardt
“I’m a gym rat and this treatment works wonders at refreshing my scalp and warding off any odors from forming between washes. I also really love the packaging. It’s supereasy to use thanks to the convenient spray nozzle.”—Giancarlos Kunhardt, associate visuals editor
Best Scalp Serum

trend_name: Cécred Restoring Hair & Edge Drops
description:
Ulta Beauty
Why It Won:For strands that feel a little fragile, whether it’s from high-tension or hormones, stress or over-styling,Cécred Restoring Hair & Edge Dropsgo right to the root of the issue to improve density and reduce breakage. The nongreasy, fast-absorbing serum supports the scalp with five different types of peptides and repairs hair follicles with protein-rich bioactive keratin ferment and fermented rice powder. It’s become anirreplaceablestep in our testers’ nightly hair regimen.
Tester feedback from editor in chiefJessica Cruel
“A year ago, I started to notice that my edges seemed to snap with even the slightest slick back. So I put away the edge brush and picked up these Cécred drops. I'm now on my second bottle. It feels like a serum—not so watery that my natural hair reverts, but not too oily either. The smell is divine, and after months of using it, I feel my hairline has become more resilient.”—Jessica Cruel, editor in chief

trend_name: Best Leave-In Conditioner
description: Best Leave-In Conditioner

trend_name: Lush Super Milk Conditioning Hair Primer
description:
Associate beauty director Sarah Kinonen applies theLush Super Milk Conditioning Hair Primer.
Why It Won:Oat, almond, coconut.Lush Super Milk Conditioning Hair Primercontains more milk options than a hipster coffee shop—all in the name of serving up a hefty dose of moisture to fried and frayed strands. Use it after the shower to detangle and decrease frizz, prior to blow-drying to protect from the heat, or as the liquid in your L.O.C. method.
Tester feedback from associate beauty directorSarah Kinonen
“Out of all of the products I tested for this year’s awards, this deliciously scented leave-in is the one I’ve restocked the most. It’s creamy but lightweight, reminds me of a cozy chai latte, and makes my bright blonde strands feel more like silk than straw."—Sarah Kinonen, associate beauty director

trend_name: Best Hair Mask
description: Best Hydrating Hair Mask

trend_name: Kerasilk Smoothing Mask
description:
Why It Won:We know it’s in the name, but we didn’t thinkKerasilk Smoothing Maskcouldactuallymake our hair feel as slick as silk. Until we were rinsing it out in the shower and running our fingers effortlessly through our hair, that is. The results aren’t just surface-level, either. Hydrolyzed proteins replenish lost amino acids that intensively nourish and maintain hair in a healthy, balanced state.
Tester feedback from senior copy manager Leslie Lipton
“Hair masks are fabulous for repairing damage and dryness, but they usually leave my fine, highlighted hair very flat and lifeless. But Kerasilk Smoothing Mask is now part of my everything shower, restoring my hair's shine and taming the frizz, giving bendability where breakability used to be."—Leslie Lipton, senior copy manager
Best Repairing Hair Mask

trend_name: Isima Súperbomba Triple Repair Peptide Mask
description:
Ulta Beauty
Why It Won:Just like Shakira’s hips, her cosmetic formulators didn’t lie when they told her theIsima Súperbomba Triple Repair Peptide Maskwould be a hit. For hair that’s been through the ringer, the treatment mask repairs damage from root to tip, the inside out, and the outside in—regardless of hair type or texture. Targeting damage brought on by both heat styling and bleaching, the mask rebuilds broken hydrogen and ionic bonds, replaces lost proteins and keratin, and seals the cuticle for a glossy, frizz-free finish.
Tester feedback from social media managerBianca Richards
“I grew up using traditional Dominican conditioning remedies to deeply nourish my hair, and this mask absolutely holds its weight against my go-to at-home staples. After just one use, my curls are brought back to life with so much shine and definition, without needing tons of product to maintain them. It’s easily one of the best new additions to my hair-care routine.”—Bianca Richards, social media manager

trend_name: Best Hair-Styling Products
description: Best Curl Cream

trend_name: Fenty Hair The Homecurl Curl-Defining Cream
description:
Fenty Beauty
Why It Won:It takes one thumb to pop openFenty Hair The Homecurl Curl-Defining Cream’sflip-up cap, two hands to rake and scrunch the thick cream through your hair, and in about three minutes, you’ve treated thirsty curls to heaps of moisture and repairing proteins so they spring enthusiastically back into shape.
Tester feedback from beauty closet associate Edward Horgan
“This is the best product for a quick, air-dry look that delivers curls without the crunch. My curls look defined, while the texture was consistently light and bouncy rather than stiff and heavy. And as for frizz, I don’t know her.”—Edward Horgan, beauty closet associate
Best Hair Gel

trend_name: Olaplex No.10 Bond Shaper Curl Defining Gel
description:
Manager of creative development Lauren Alberti applies theOlaplex No.10 Bond Shaper Curl Defining Gel.
Why It Won: Olaplex No.10 Bond Shaper Curl Defining Gelcoats the hair in flexible, humidity-resistant polymers to form structured spirals with zero frizz. And because curls have broken bonds, too, the brand infused the formula with its renowned hair-repairing bond builders as well.
Tester feedback from manager of creative development Lauren Alberti
“I unknowingly put this product to the ultimate test by going out on a humid summer afternoon and getting caught in a surprise thunderstorm. Typically, something like that would have me putting my hair up in a messy bun and giving up on it looking cute for the rest of the day. But behold! The Olaplex No.10 Bond Shaper Curl Defining Gel proved me wrong. My curls were able to quickly recover from the surprise rain and extreme humidity with this gel. I was shocked that I could keep my hair down for the rest of the day and have it not look wild and frizzy.”—Lauren Alberti, manager of creative development
Best Hair Volumizer

trend_name: K18 Biomimetic Hairscience AstroLift Reparative Volume Spray
description:
Why It Won:TheK18 Biomimetic Hairscience AstroLift Reparative Volume Spraytakes limp hair (especially fine, limp hair), up up and…. up even higher with an extract of palmaria palmata, a seaweed that’s rich in the amino acid arginine that helps thicken up individual strands. The brand’s signature protein-repairing peptide simultaneously takes a journey into the individual hair shaft to repair any damage, helping tackle a lack of volume on a long-term basis.
Tester feedback from features directorDianna Singh
“I've tried a lot of volumizers in my fine-haired day, and I've found a handful that deliver oomph immediately—but very few that add volume that lasts. This spray is one of those rare gems: I apply it in damp hair, blow-dry, and then enjoy the extra bounce well into the following day.”—Dianna Singh, features director
Best Hair Texturizer

trend_name: Maria Nila Texture Spray
description:
Why It Won:Our testers are still wondering howMaria Nila Texture Spraycan be both the sheerest mist they’ve tried and also the one that adds the most body, dimension, and grip. What they’re not questioning? That their hair gets better with every spritz of this delicate dry styler.
Tester feedback from social directorKassidy Silva
"A texturizing dream! Two quick spritzes to each side of my part keeps my roots lifted, and it adds the extra grip I need to keep my claw clip in place all day."—Kassidy Silva, social director
Best Wave Enhancer

trend_name: Ouai Wave Spray
description:
Why It Won:Surfer-style beach waves, French-girl bed head, rumpled rocker vibes— whatever type of effortless bends you're after, theOuai Wave Spraycan coax them out of the straightest of strands. And while the musky-floral fragrance brings major beach vibes, the nonsticky styler skips the sea salt, replacing it with hydrolyzed rice protein to add volume and natural movement minus the crunchiness or tangles.
Tester feedback from commerce producerSarah Hoffmann
“This is my second-day hair savior. I use a mister bottle to dampen my hair, then essentially douse it in this wave spray, and it consistently turns out perfect. It adds just enough shape and volume that I feel put-together even on air-dry days.”—Sarah Hoffmann, commerce producer
Best Curl Enhancer

trend_name: Curlsmith Curl Defining Styling Soufflé
description:
Why It Won:If this were a beauty pageant and not theBestof Beauty,Curlsmith Curl Defining Styling Souffléwould take home the award for Miss Congeniality. The silky gel gets along with every curl type (our testers ranged from 2A to 3C), encasing spirals of all patterns in a pliable cast that helps keep them buoyant. And as if we needed another reason to like it, the formula flushes curls with rich-but-lightweight moisture in the form of shea butter, linseed seed extract, and more than a few oils (avocado, sunflower, and andiroba, a plant from the Amazon rainforest).
Tester feedback from senior manager of analytics and audience development Monica Perry
“I've seen beauty creators from 2C–4C curls test this gel, and while the results are varying degrees of definition, depending on the style and the curl pattern, it always seemed to deeply moisturize without significant shrinkage. But I was still a bit skeptical (because I’m always skeptical) when I tried it on my mixed curl pattern. 10/10. After my Best of Beauty testing bottle ran out, I bought it in bulk.”—Monica Perry, senior manager of analytics and audience development
Best Finishing Oil

trend_name: Wella Professionals Ultimate Smooth Miracle Oil Serum
description:
Why It Won:Both our fine- and thick-haired testers marvelled at the exceptional luster left behind byWella Professionals Ultimate Smooth Miracle Oil Serum. What’s truly miraculous? The fact that they found themselves in total agreement over the ultra-lightweight shine-booster and frizz-smoother.
Tester feedback from Best of Beauty editorial assistant ElleAnna Revell
“I have loose, wavy hair that tends to get frizzy, so I like using this oil post air-drying. I evenly distribute one to two pumps by raking my fingers through my strands; no styling is necessary. It leaves my hair feeling unbelievably soft and so shiny, and I like that the oil absorbs so fast that a greasy residue is never left behind,”—ElleAnna Revell, Best of Beauty editorial assistant
Best Heat Protector

trend_name: Drybar Hot Toddy Heat Protectant Mist
description:
Why It Won:With over 150 blowout-only salons around the country,Drybarknows a thing or two about heat and hair, and theHot Toddy Heat Protectant Mistproves it. You can rely on it to effectively—and weightlessly—shield strands from color-fading UV rays and hair-singeing temperatures of up to 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
Tester feedback from senior copy manager Leslie Lipton
“Other heat protectants can actually make my hair staticky, but the lightweight marula oil in Drybar Hot Toddy Heat Protectant Mist coats just enough to keep hot tools from literally frying my strands.”—Leslie Lipton, senior copy manager
Best Multitasker

trend_name: Chris McMillan The Styling Balm
description:
Why It Won:LongtimeAllurereaders will remember that hairstylist Chris McMillan shared his tips and tricks on the pages ofAllurefor over a decade. Now he’s bottled them up in the form ofThe Styling Balm,a multitasking, serum-like balm that delivers heat protection, definition, and exceptional smoothness—whether your style of choice is a Rachel-reminiscent shag or a “c*nty little bob.” Our testers even reached for it on air-dry days to add texture and shine.
Tester Feedback from senior copy manager Leslie Lipton
“Chris McMillan The Styling Balm is a new star in my hair-care routine. I apply it to freshly washed, towel-dried hair before blow-drying and a bit more when I'm done to seal and smooth my ends (although if you like piecey texture, it can work that way, too).”—Leslie Lipton, senior copy manager
Best Styling Cream

trend_name: Bumble & Bumble Bb.Hairdresser’s Invisible Oil Long Last Styling Cream
description:
Commerce writer Lily Wohlner applies theBumble & Bumble Bb.Hairdresser’s Invisible Oil Long Last Styling Cream.
Why It Won: Bumble & Bumble Bb.Hairdresser’s Invisible Oil Long Last Styling Creammakes fuzzies and flyaways disappear when smoothed over a sleek chignon or slicked-back ponytail. But this is more than just a finishing product. Used as a prep step, it protects from heat and adds polish to a blow-out or air-dry, its seven different oils (nangai, coconut, argan, grape seed, macadamia, sweet almond, and safflower seed) ensuring you get shape without any stiffness.
Tester feedback from commerce writerLily Wohlner
“My hair is on the finer side, so I can tell immediately when a product is too heavy. This cream never weighs my strands down but still delivers on moisture and hydration. It’s also a powerhouse when it comes to fighting frizz and humidity in the summer, which my hair is always in dire need of. Think of it as a frizz-fighting styling cream and smoothing hair oil rolled into one.”—Lily Wohlner, commerce writer
Best Styling Cream

trend_name: RŌZ Evergreen Style Cream
description:
Why It Won:It should come as no surprise that a hairstylist known for creating effortless hairstyles (hello, Emma Stone’s whimsy pixie) has created such an easy-to-use styling product. Mara Roszak developed theRŌZ Evergreen Style Creamto use in place of traditional gels, providing light-to-medium hold with shea butter, hydrolyzed pea protein, and carnauba wax. A little goes a long way to get what you want from this does-it-all styler—and it really does it all, from Emma Stone’s feathery pixie to Zoe Saldaña’s sleek Met Gala bob (both Roszak clients).
Tester feedback from shopping directorShanna Shipin
“I wear my hair in so many ways—naturally curly, bouncy blowout, sleek middle part—and this cream elevates my look, no matter how I choose to style it. It can define curls, smooth heat-styled ends, and lockdown flyaways for updos. This cream is like lotion, sealing up dry ends, taming frizz, and making it all look supple and healthy. It doesn't hurt that its fragrance profile is subtle and elegant, like you're taking a drive through Big Sur with the windows down, and happened to pick up some of the magic in your hair.”—Shanna Shipin, shopping director
Best Hairspray

trend_name: L’Oréal Paris Elnett Satin Extra Strong Hold HairSpray
description:
Why It Won:Taking center stage once again in this category is theL’Oréal Paris Elnett Satin Extra Strong Hold Hairspray.Beloved by professional dancers and hairstylists alike, the iconic, extra-fine mist performs beautifully whether you’re shellacking a ballerina bun in place or creating a blow-dry you can still brush through.
Tester feedback from contributing writerSophia Panych
“As someone who took ballet since they were four years old, I've had a can of Elnett in my bathroom since middle school. When I became a beauty editor covering fashion week, I was shocked to learn my favorite hairspray was a backstage staple, and discovered a whole new way to use it. Now, not only do I still reach for the stuff to keep slicked-back styles pristine, but I also use it to set soft waves or a smooth blowout and have them stay touchable.”—Sophia Panych, contributing writer
Best Hair Mousse

trend_name: Lottabody Wrap Me Foaming Mousse
description:
Why It Won:Coconut and shea butter oils are two of the heaviest moisturizers out there, so it’s mind-boggling howLottabody Wrap Me Foaming Mousseproduces such airy volume and bouncy curls when raked through wet hair. Perhaps not so surprising? The level of shine and suppleness that those nourishing oils impart.
Tester feedback from creative director Amber Venerable
“Whether I have a black pixie, long braids, or a platinum short curly cut, I use this foam almost every day. It smells great and foams up the perfect amount I like for any and all hairstyles. Most importantly, however, it doesn’t dry the hair out."—Amber Venerable, creative director
Best Pomade

trend_name: Fatboy Perfect Putty
description:
Senior news editor Nicola Dall’Asen applies theFatboy Perfect Putty.
Why It Won:The kaolin clay inFatboy Perfect Puttymay add grit to the hair, but the beeswax and carnauba wax mean the paste feels soft and creamy—not icky sticky. Malleable and with a matte finish, when worked through the hair, this white paste keeps flyaways at bay, musses up the ends of short cuts, and sculpts amazing second-day texture.
Tester feedback from senior news editorNicola Dall’Asen
“As a short-haired girl with perpetual flyaways, I'll try any hair pomade once. Most of them, I only try once because I'm that damn picky. Fatboy's Perfect Putty, though, has made it back into my stash time and again since a hairstylist introduced me to it nearly 10 years ago—and I always use up every last bit until I'm desperately scraping the corners of the jar. It's perfect to me: matte enough to tamp down oily sheens but not so much that it dries my hair out; heavy enough to weigh down flyaways and keep them down for hours on end but not enough to flatten out volume; soft and pliable enough to work through hair easily but grippy enough to keep everything in its place.”—Nicola Dall’Asen, senior news editor
Best Edge Control

trend_name: Creme of Nature Argan Oil from Morocco Perfect Edges Firm Hold Edge Control
description:
Creative producer Sydney Malone applies theCreme of Nature Argan Oil from Morocco Perfect Edges Firm Hold Edge Control.
Why It Won:It’s not just that theCreme of Nature Argan Oil from Morocco Perfect Edges Firm Hold Edge Controlallows you to swirl and sculpt your hairline to your heart’s desire. It’s that the moisturizing gel keeps the most stubborn edges in place for up to 24 hours, laying them down precisely with lots of shine and zero flakes. And regardless of whether your hair is naturally kinky or relaxed, you can trust that your hair will smell heavenly and remain safe from humidity.
Tester feedback from creative producer Sydney Malone
“I have super coiled, natural hair, so finding an edge control that actually holds all day, especially in the summer, is tough. I’ve been using Creme of Nature Argan Oil from Morocco’s Perfect Edges Firm Hold Edge Control for years because it really keeps my edges laid without flaking or giving up halfway through the day. I usually wrap my hair for 15 to 20 minutes after applying it, and that little step makes a big difference. It’s become my go-to—whether I’m running errands or getting ready for a big event.”—Sydney Malone, creative producer
Best Twisting Cream

trend_name: SheaMoisture Kalahari Melon Oil Curl Stretch Multi-Tasking Volume Cream
description:
Why It Won:Melon and coconut sound like the makings of a delicious tropical treat, but they’re also what allows theSheaMoisture Kalahari Melon Oil Curl Stretch Multi-Tasking Volume Creamto define and elongate type 4 coils so effectively. Shea butter and hyaluronic acid join coconut and Kalahari melon oils to add a surge of strand-softening hydration, minimizing shrinkage in the process.
Tester feedback from editor in chiefJessica Cruel
“Slipping and sliding! That is what my coils were doing after I applied this cream. It has amazing slip that helped detangleandbrought every individual curl to life. It works best when applied to wet hair for either a wash-and-go or twist out. Bonus points: Use a dryer to set the length instead of air-drying. It's got a soft hold for touchable definition.”—Jessica Cruel, editor in chief
Best Dry Shampoo Spray

trend_name: Batiste Light Dry Shampoo Mellow Melon
description:
Ulta Beauty
What It Won:It’s hard to improve upon an award-winning original, butBatiste Light Dry Shampoo Mellow Melonmanaged to—and then some. Rice starch still sops up oil and beefs up strands, but with an even airier spray that stays invisible and smells softly fruity and floral.
Tester feedback from content directorKara McGrath
“I have professed my love for Batiste Original Dry Shampoo countless times, but even I understand that, for some, the highly effective aerosol-propelled powder can be a bit intense. If the OG dry shampoo leaves your hair feeling a little too gritty, you'll likely love the new ‘light’ version. I find it soaks up oil just as well, but it doesn't leave as much of a trace at the roots, and the scent is a bit lighter.”—Kara McGrath, content director
Best Dry Shampoo Powder

trend_name: Ceremonia Dry Shampoo con Arrowroot
description:
Features director Dianna Singh apples theCeremonia Dry Shampoo con Arrowroot.
Why It Won:If you prefer a nonaerosol dry shampoo (that’s also talc- and alcohol- free),Ceremonia Dry Shampoo con Arrowrootdispenses its oil-absorbing rice starch and arrowroot powder throughout your hair with apffftof air. Then, watch as your greasy roots—and the product itself—disappear.
Tester feedback from features directorDianna Singh
“Ceremonia Dry Shampoo con Arrowroot has a pouf applicator that makes it easy to target roots. And the formula itself both absorbs excess oil and adds just the right amount of volume and texture.”—Dianna Singh, features director

trend_name: Best At-Home Hair Color & Dye
description: Best Permanent Hair Color

trend_name: eSalon Custom Hair Color Set
description:
Why It Won:If you’ve ever wished you could have a hairstylist by your side when dyeing your hair at home,eSalon Custom Hair Color Setis the next best thing. The deliver-to-your-door system’s probably the most personalized box dye that exists, hence its 10 Best of Beauty wins over the years. Fill out a questionnaire, upload a photo, and a professional colorist mixes your perfect blend based on your hair goals and history—right down to the developer strength. The only thing you won’t get is the in-salon gossip session.
Tester feedback from contributor Liana Schaffner
“The finished result was so flattering and natural, it was like the shade was made for me—because it was.”—Liana Schaffner, contributor
Best Semi-Permanent Hair Color

trend_name: Good Dye Young DYEposit
description:
Why It Won:TheGood Dye Young DYEpositprovides casual hair color for commitment-phobes and DIYers with a formula that lets you switch up or enhance your hue in the shower. In shades that range from subdued and natural to shocking and rock-star rebellious (this is Paramore lead singer Hayley Williams’ brand after all), just replace your conditioner with the treatment-slash-temporary-tint once a week, and you’ll emerge with vivid color and significantly softer strands.
Tester feedback from senior news editorNicola Dall’Asen
“Take it from someone who's been bleaching and dyeing their hair à la Hayley Williams for 15 years now: DYEposit is a nonnegotiable for upkeep when you've got brightly colored hair. It doesn't just keep vivid hair colors saturated for months on end; it works like a conditioning mask, too, helping to undo all the damage you probably did dyeing it in the first place."—Nicola Dall’Asen, senior news editor
Best Clear Hair Gloss

trend_name: JVN Revive Rapid Repair Bond Gloss
description:
Why It Won:As editors, we prefer not to gloss over things, except when it comes to a product likeJVN Revive Rapid Repair Bond Gloss.Exceptionally thorough, the clear, universal formula doesn’t just impart a salon-worthy sheen. In the 60 seconds you leave it on in the shower, strands are treated to a strengthening and shine-enhancing solution of bond builders, rice protein, and algae-derived sugars.
Tester feedback from beauty directorJenny Bailly
“My hair is bleached, and gray, and growing out a body that has been on this earth for almost five decades now—it’s definitely seen shinier days. But a single minute in the shower with this clear gloss brings it back (or at least way closer) to the days before balayage and fading pigment and perimenopause.”—Jenny Bailly, executive beauty director
Best Color Hair Gloss

trend_name: dpHue Glossy Glaze
description:
Why It Won:The saying goes that there are only three things you can be certain of—life, death, and fading hair color. Or maybe it was taxes? Regardless, the reality of getting your hair dyed or highlighted is that it will eventually start to go dull. That’s whydpHue Glossy Glazeis so handy. Available in seven shades (cool blonde, golden blonde, light to medium brown, caramel, dark brown, copper, and sheer) and applied in the shower after you shampoo, the tinted formula needs just two minutes to revive your color back to its original dazzling and dimensional shape.
Tester feedback from Best of Beauty editorial assistant ElleAnna Revell
“My hair tends to get dull and dry, especially during the colder months. This Glossy Glaze is my secret two-minute fix for silky, shiny strands. I use this product every couple of weeks in the shower after shampooing, and it genuinely looks like I just left the salon!”—ElleAnna Revell, Best of Beauty editorial assistant
Best Powder Root Coverage

trend_name: Madison Reed The Great Cover Up Root Touch Up + Brow Filler
description:
Why It Won:Raking in its fourth Best of Beauty Award, it's clear we might have some attachment issues with our handyMadison Reed The Great Cover Up Root Touch Up + Brow Filler.We don’t ever want to be without this convenient compact, which is the best quick fix for concealing grays, camouflaging regrowth, and filling in brows. Each of the six shades looks completely natural and won’t abandon you until you’re ready to wash it out.
Tester feedback from executive beauty directorJenny Bailly
“I love my balayage sessions with my longtime colorist Abby Haliti—but I still only manage to get in her chair every three or four months. To brighten and blend in those last few weeks before an appointment, nothing beats this budgeproof powder—I can attest that it survives every sudden rainshower or sweaty New York City day.”—Jenny Bailly, executive beauty director
Best Spray Root Coverage

trend_name: Clairol Root Touch-Up 2in1 Color + Volume Spray
description:
Why It Won:Hair colorist on holiday? TheClairol Root Touch-Up 2in1 Color + Volume Sprayhelps you hold out until they get back. The spray, which feels a bit like dry shampoo, seamlessly covers grays and regrowth, with the added benefit of bulking up your hairline.
Tester feedback from contributing writerTalia Gutierrez
"I can't stop tying my hair up in a tight, slick-backed ponytail. I love the sleek style, but wearing it as often as I do has caused some thinning at my hairline. I'm self-conscious about it, so I spray a bit of this root cover-up on areas where damage is most noticeable. Not only does it give my hairline a fuller look, but it also covers up incoming (and incredibly stubborn) gray hairs near my temples."—Talia Gutierrez, contributing writer
Written by Sophia Panych
To determine theBest of Beauty Hair winners,Allureeditors and contributors test thousands of hair-care and styling products over many months, evaluating each formula for its ingredients, efficacy, texture, packaging, fragrance (when relevant), and performance.
Testing hair-care products is different from categories like makeup, body-care, and fragrance. There are products that can be used on different textures, lengths, curl patterns, thicknesses, colors (natural and unnatural), and needs, and in those cases we call on several editors with different hair types to gauge if those formulas do indeed work for everyone.
Often, though, hair products are created with specific consumers in mind. Sometimes this means a product is formulated to address a particular concern (dandruff, breakage, brittleness) or to work most effectively for a specific hair type (4C curls, wavy hair, gray hair). In these cases we enlist testers who represent our readers most likely to use that product so we can best evaluate the formula and determine if it is truly award-winning.
Yes. Many of theBest of Beauty Hair winnersare color-safe and/or designed to repair and strengthen damaged strands. You’ll find bond-building treatments, deep-conditioning masks, nourishing glosses, and gentle shampoos that help maintain vibrancy, reduce breakage, and support chemically processed or over-styled hair.
The products spotlighted in theBest of Beauty Hair categoryaddress the full spectrum of textures and concerns. This means that whether your hair is curly, coily, coarse, straight, wavy, thinning, thick, or color-treated, you’ll find an award-winning product that can upgrade and enhance your existing routine.
Yes. The winners span a full spectrum of price points, from budget-friendly staples available at the drugstore (L’Oréal Paris Elnett Satin Extra Strong Hold Hairspray, for instance) to premium products from renowned hairstylists (looking at you, Chris McMillan Styling Balm). For even more options, you can find our favorite products of the year under $24 in our list ofSteals winnersand explore the most impressive indulgent beauty buys of 2025 in ourSplurges winners.
This category, which features 46 products total, encompasses almost every kind of hair-care and styling product imaginable. This means the list of winners includes a broad range of options, such as clarifying shampoo, edge control, curl cream, texturizing spray, volumizing mousse, leave-in conditioner, dry shampoo, root touch-ups, and much, much more.
